channel.title: on Jacob Nibauer
channel.description: Recent content in on Jacob Nibauer

item.title: Research
item.description: A selection of research projects I have led Statistical Properties of Elemental Abundances in Solar Analogs and Possible Links to Planet Formation with Eric Baxter (Hawaii, IfA), Bhuvnesh Jain (UPenn), Jennifer van Saders (Hawaii, IfA), Rachael Beaton (Princeton, Carnegie Institution for Science), Johanna Teske (Carnegie Institution of Washington) arxiv: 2010.07241 | Accepted for Publication in the Astrophysical Journal Recent studies have demonstrated that the Sun appears depleted in refractory (rock-forming) elements by ~10% relative to nearby solar-type stars.

item.title: Recitation Material
item.description: Updated weekly. Back Vol. 1, Ch. 1-9 | Solutions Vol. 1, Ch. 10-15 | Solutions Vol. 1, Ch. 16-18, Vol. 2, Ch. 1-3 | Solutions

item.title: Teaching
item.description: A collection of my recitation problems and handwritten lecture notes for Math 114 (multivariable calculus) at Penn can be found here. Spring 2020 Recitations: Monday DRL 4E19, 8-8:50am | 9-9:50am Friday DRL 2C4, 9-9:50am Recitation worksheets will be uploaded on a weekly basis here Office hours: Monday DRL 4C9, 2:00-3:30pm Wednesday DRL 4C9, 2-3pm Friday DRL 4E9, 4:30-6pm (Weekly Review Session) Math 114E: Multivariable Calculus for Engineers This page can also be accessed with the url https://tinyurl.
